cards.q: ما هو موضوع الفصل السابع في إدارة قواعد بيانات أوراكل؟
cards.a: المستخدمون والأمن الأساسي (Users and Basic Security) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: عند إنشاء وإدارة حسابات مستخدم قاعدة البيانات، ما الذي يجب تعيينه بالإضافة إلى الحساب نفسه؟
cards.a: مناطق التخزين الافتراضية (tablespaces) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: اذكر أحد الأهداف المتعلقة بإدارة الصلاحيات في قواعد بيانات أوراكل.
cards.a: منح وإلغاء الامتيازات (Grant and revoke privileges) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: ما هي الإجراءات التي تهدف إلى تعزيز الأمن والتحكم في استخدام الموارد للمستخدمين؟
cards.a: تطبيق ميزات أمن كلمة المرور القياسية، أو التحكم في استخدام الموارد بواسطة المستخدمين (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What key tasks are involved in creating and managing database user accounts?
cards.a: Authenticating users and assigning default storage areas (tablespaces). (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: What administrative functions relate to controlling database permissions?
cards.a: Granting and revoking privileges, and creating and managing roles. (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: What two main areas do profiles help control for database users?
cards.a: Implementing standard password security features and controlling resource usage by users. (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: Name the four key administrative tasks covered when managing database users.
cards.a: Creating/managing user accounts; granting/revoking privileges; creating/managing roles; and creating/managing profiles. (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: What is a privilege in an Oracle database?
cards.a: A right to execute a particular type of SQL statement or to access another user's object. (Page 9)

cards.q: How is a role defined regarding privileges?
cards.a: A named group of related privileges that are granted to users or to other roles. (Page 9)

cards.q: What does the SYSDBA privilege allow an administrator to do?
cards.a: It allows an administrator to connect to the SYS account by using the AS SYSDBA clause. (Page 7)

cards.q: What are Profiles used for in database administration?
cards.a: They impose a named set of resource limits on database usage and instance resources, and manage account status and password management rules. (Page 9)

cards.q: What is a schema in a database?
cards.a: A collection of database objects that are owned by a database user and has the same name as the user account. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: What does a user profile assign to a user?
cards.a: A set of resource and password restrictions assigned to the user. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: Which account status indicates that the lifetime has expired, preventing connection until the password is reset?
cards.a: EXPIRED (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: What are the constraints for a unique username in Oracle Database?
cards.a: Usernames cannot exceed 30 bytes, cannot contain special characters, and must start with a letter. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: What does the SYS account own and what is it used for?
cards.a: It owns the data dictionary and the Automatic Workload Repository (AWR), and is used for startup and shutdown of the database instance. (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: Which predefined administrative accounts facilitate specific operations?
cards.a: SYSBACKUP facilitates Oracle Recovery Manager (RMAN) backup and recovery operations, SYSDG facilitates Oracle Data Guard operations, and SYSKM facilitates Transparent Data Encryption wallet operations. (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: To connect to the SYS account, what clauses must be used for a database instance and an Automatic Storage Management (ASM) instance?
cards.a: AS SYSDBA clause for a database instance and AS SYSASM for an Automatic Storage Management (ASM) instance. (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: Which "privileged" users are allowed to start up and shut down instances?
cards.a: Only users who are granted the SYSDBA, SYSOPER, or SYSASM privileges. (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: What accounts are required in the database and cannot be dropped?
cards.a: The SYS and SYSTEM accounts (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: Which users are created to facilitate separation of duties for database administrators?
cards.a: SYSBACKUP, SYSDG, and SYSKM users (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: What does the SYSDEA privilege allow for?
cards.a: Standard database operations, such as starting and shutting down the database instance, creating the server parameter file (SPFILE), and changing the ARCHIVELOG mode. (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: According to security best practices, what should be done minimally when securing a database?
cards.a: Change the password for every default account and then lock any accounts that are not using. (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: After creating a database, what security measures are recommended for default accounts?
cards.a: It is better to lock every default account and change their passwords; and to unlock default users only as they are needed. (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: What SQL command is used to unlock a locked user account?
cards.a: `alter user outlin account unlock` (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: When does Oracle prompt a user for a new password if the current one has expired?
cards.a: The user is prompted when first connecting to the database as that user. (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: Which view should be checked to determine whether any accounts are still set to default passwords?
cards.a: `DBA_USERS_WITH_DEFPWD` (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: When creating a user, what factors must be considered?
cards.a: Username and authentication method, Basic privileges, Default permanent tablespace and space quotas, and Default temporary tablespace (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: In pluggable database environments, how are common users and local users defined?
cards.a: Common users span all pluggable databases within a container database. Local users exist within one pluggable database (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: What essential data must be entered when creating a new user account?
cards.a: User name, authentication method, and profile (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: Besides the username and authentication method, what storage assignments are required for a new user?
cards.a: Default permanent tablespace and space quotas, and Default temporary tablespace (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: What process is used to assign roles to a new user?
cards.a: Granting roles (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: How are system privileges granted to a new user?
cards.a: By granting system privileges (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: What is the procedure for giving specific permissions on an object to a user?
cards.a: Granting object privileges (الصفحة 27)

cards.q: What are profiles used for in Oracle database administration?
cards.a: To impose a named set of resource limits on database users and instance resources (الصفحة 27)

cards.q: What is an authentication method for a user account?
cards.a: Some means whereby the database can determine if the user attempting to create a session connecting to the account is allowed to do so. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: Which administrators use Operating System Authentication, and what groups control these connections?
cards.a: Administrators who log in as SYSDBA or as SYSOPER; the controlling groups are OSDBA and OSOPER. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: What is required for External authentication and Global authentication types?
cards.a: They require an LDAP directory server, which is the Oracle Internet Directory. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: Name the five types of authentication methods available to a user account.
cards.a: Operating system authentication, Password file authentication, Password authentication, External authentication, and Global authentication. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: What type of administrator authentication logs in as SYSDBA or as SYSOPER, requiring users to know their Oracle username and password?
cards.a: Password File Authentication (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: To enable Password File Authentication for remote login, what command must be set?
cards.a: SET REMOTE_LOGIN_PASSWORDFILE = EXCLUSIVE (or SHARED if database is part of distributed system) (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: After creating a new Oracle user, how are SYSDBA or SYSOPER privileges assigned to that user?
cards.a: GRANT sysdba TO student; (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What is the security risk associated with Password File Authentication due to password storage?
cards.a: Possible security risk due to storage of passwords in an encrypted operating system file. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What do System privileges primarily apply to?
cards.a: Actions that affect the data dictionary, such as creating tables or users; or actions affecting the database or the instance (Page 36)

cards.q: What types of commands can Object privileges grant against tables and related objects?
cards.a: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE commands against tables and related objects, and to execute PL/SQL objects (Page 36)

cards.q: How are user privileges managed in an Oracle database?
cards.a: Privileges are assigned to user accounts with a GRANT command and withdrawn with a REVOKE (Page 34)

cards.q: What function does the GRANT OPTION provide regarding object privileges?
cards.a: It lets a user pass his/her object privilege on to a third party (Page 36)

cards.q: What SQL statement is used to revoke system privileges that were granted directly?
cards.a: REVOKE SQL statement (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: Which users can revoke a system privilege if they possess ADMIN OPTION?
cards.a: Users with ADMIN OPTION for a system privilege can revoke the privilege from any other database user (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: Must the revoker of a system privilege be the same user who originally granted it?
cards.a: No, the revoker does not have to be the same user who originally granted the privilege (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: What is true regarding cascading effects when a system privilege is revoked?
cards.a: There are no cascading effects when a system privilege is revoked, regardless of whether it was given the ADMIN OPTION (الصفحة 38)

cards.q: When revoking a system privilege related to DML operations, what must happen if a user has created procedures using that table?
cards.a: All procedures contained in the user's schema must be recompiled before they can be used again. (الصفحة 40)

cards.q: When revoking object privileges given with GRANT OPTION, who is authorized to revoke those privileges?
cards.a: Only the grantee or a user with the privilege called GRANT ANY OBJECT PRIVILEGE. (الصفحة 40)

cards.q: What is defined as a role in Oracle databases?
cards.a: A named group of related privileges that are granted to users or to other roles. (الصفحة 42)

cards.q: Regarding their ownership and location, what statement about roles is true?
cards.a: Roles are not owned by anyone, and they are not in any schema. (الصفحة 42)

cards.q: ما هو عنوان دليل مسؤول قاعدة بيانات أوراكل الذي نُشر في مارس 2019؟
cards.a: Oracle Database Administrator's Guide, 18c (Oracle University) (الصفحة 43)

cards.q: ما هو الكتاب الذي يتناول إدارة قاعدة بيانات أوراكل في السحابة؟
cards.a: The Cloud DBA- Oracle: Managing Oracle Database in the Cloud – First Edition (الصفحة 43)

cards.q: اذكر أحد المواقع الإلكترونية الأساسية للتوثيق الخاص بقاعدة بيانات أوراكل.
cards.a: https://docs.oracle.com/database (الصفحة 43)

cards.q: ما هو الرابط المخصص لمعرفة ميزات "whats-new" في وثائق قاعدة بيانات أوراكل؟
cards.a: https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/19/whats-new.html (الصفحة 43)
